__comment: EuroTransit RED dashboard — one row per RED signal across the five services, plus a checkout (money-path) row and the Kafka pipeline lag. RED = Rate, Errors, Duration, measured from the caller-visible HTTP metrics (Micrometer http_server_requests). Answers: is the money path healthy right now, and if not, which service?
title: EuroTransit — RED (money path)

panels.title: Rate — requests/s per service
panels.description: R of RED. sum(rate) of http_server_requests per service, actuator excluded.

panels.title: Errors — % 5xx per service
panels.description: E of RED. Symptom signal: user-visible failures, not CPU.

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (job) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{namespace="eurotransit", status=~"5..", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[2m])) / sum by (job) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{namespace="eurotransit", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[2m]))

panels.title: Duration — p95 per service
panels.description: D of RED. Watch the tail: p95 is what users feel. Catches 'slow death' (timing failures that raise no error).

panels.title: Kafka pipeline — consumer records lag
panels.description: The async half of the money path. Rising lag = a consumer cannot keep up (or is down): backpressure signal. Should spike and drain during chaos CE-2, and stay ~0 in steady state.

panels.title: Checkout success rate (1h)
panels.description: SLI for the success-rate SLO (POST /orders, non-5xx). Ratified target (D2, docs/design/slo-definitions.md): >= 99%. Red = SLO violated, yellow = meeting the SLO with thin headroom. 429 (load shedding) counts as success by design.

targets.expr: 100 * (1 - (sum(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{namespace="eurotransit", job="eurotransit-orders", uri="/orders", method="POST", status=~"5.."}[1h])) or vector(0)) / sum(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{namespace="eurotransit", job="eurotransit-orders", uri="/orders", method="POST"}[1h])))

panels.title: Checkout p95 (5m)
panels.description: SLI for the latency SLO. Ratified target (D2): p95 < 500 ms (matches the CheckoutHighP95Latency alert threshold); yellow from 350 ms = approaching the SLO.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{namespace="eurotransit", job="eurotransit-orders", uri="/orders", method="POST"}[5m])))

panels.title: Checkout rate — POST /orders
panels.description: Money-path entry traffic. During a canary, compare stable vs canary track here before promoting.
